name: Jamil Abdullah Al-Amin
description: Imam and former chairman of the Student Nonviolent Coordinating Committee (SNCC) and Justice Minister of the Black Panther Party — known in the 1960s civil rights and Black liberation movements as H. Rap Brown. After leaving the BPP he converted to Islam and led the Dar ul-Islam Movement and later the Atlanta-based Community Mosque. Convicted in March 2002 of the March 16, 2000 killing of Fulton County, Georgia sheriff's deputy Ricky Kinchen and the wounding of deputy Aldranon English. Maintains his innocence; another man, Otis Jackson, signed a notarized confession to the shooting. Sentenced to life without parole. Held at USP Tucson, then at the federal supermax ADX Florence, and as of 2021 at FMC Butner battling advanced multiple myeloma.

cases.charges: Murder of Fulton County sheriff's deputy Ricky Kinchen; aggravated assault on deputy Aldranon English

name: Jessica Reznicek
description: Catholic Worker peace activist and water protector from the Des Moines Catholic Worker community. With co-defendant Ruby Montoya, conducted a months-long sabotage campaign against the Dakota Access Pipeline in 2016-17 — burning construction equipment, dismantling water valves, and piercing empty pipeline sections with oxyacetylene torches. Publicly took responsibility for the actions in a July 2017 press conference. Pleaded guilty September 2020 to one count of conspiracy to damage an energy facility; sentenced June 30, 2021 to 8 years in federal prison plus 3 years supervised release and $3,198,512 in restitution, with the court applying a domestic-terrorism sentencing enhancement.

cases.charges: Conspiracy to damage an energy facility (18 U.S.C. § 1366)
cases.sentence: 8 years federal prison + 3 years supervised release + $3.2M restitution (terrorism enhancement)

name: Daniel Alan Baker
description: Anti-fascist activist and U.S. Army veteran who went AWOL rather than deploy to Iraq, then traveled to Rojava (northern Syria) in 2017 and joined the YPG-aligned International Freedom Battalion fighting Islamic State. Arrested January 15, 2021 in Tallahassee, Florida after FBI investigation of his social-media posts calling for an armed response to fascist mobilization around the Capitol on January 20, 2021. Pleaded guilty May 2021 to two counts of transmitting interstate communications containing threats; sentenced to 44 months in federal prison plus 3 years supervised release.

cases.charges: Two counts of transmitting interstate communications containing threats (18 U.S.C. § 875(c))
cases.sentence: 44 months federal prison + 3 years supervised release

name: Urooj Rahman
description: Pakistani-American human-rights attorney from Brooklyn. With co-defendant Colinford Mattis (a Princeton-educated corporate lawyer), threw a Molotov cocktail at an unoccupied, already-damaged NYPD vehicle during the May 30, 2020 George Floyd uprising in Brooklyn. Originally charged with seven federal counts carrying up to 45 years; after years of plea negotiation, pleaded guilty October 2022 to a single count of conspiracy. Sentenced February 2023 to 15 months in federal prison plus 2 years supervised release and $30,000 in restitution. Began her sentence in 2023 and was released in 2024.

cases.charges: Conspiracy to damage a vehicle by fire (Molotov cocktail at empty NYPD vehicle, May 30 2020)
cases.sentence: 15 months federal prison + 2 years supervised release + $30,000 restitution

name: Lore-Elisabeth Blumenthal
description: Philadelphia massage therapist and community care worker for people living with HIV and chronic illness, federally prosecuted as a scapegoat for the May 30, 2020 George Floyd protests in Center City, where two unoccupied Philadelphia Police vehicles were set on fire. Identified after FBI agents traced her custom 'O' t-shirt (purchased on Etsy) and the explanatory call her mother received from a former Sandia National Labs nuclear-weapons designer who saw her on Fox News. Charged with arson of a police vehicle; pleaded guilty April 2021. Sentenced October 2021 to 4 years federal prison.

cases.charges: Arson of a Philadelphia Police vehicle (May 30, 2020)

name: Anthony Smith
description: North Philadelphia high-school social-studies teacher and longtime activist with Food Not Bombs Solidarity, Philly for REAL Justice, and the Coalition Against Death by Incarceration. Federally charged in October 2020 for alleged involvement in the burning of a Philadelphia Police cruiser at 18th and Race Streets during the May 30, 2020 George Floyd uprising. After three years on house arrest, pleaded guilty in 2023; sentenced November 2023 to one year and one day in federal prison plus 2 years probation. The conviction bars him from teaching in Pennsylvania for ten years.

cases.charges: Obstructing law enforcement during civil disorder; aiding and abetting arson of a police cruiser
cases.sentence: 1 year and 1 day federal prison + 2 years probation

name: Brian DiPippa
description: Pittsburgh anarchist and antifascist organizer. Convicted alongside his wife Krystal DiPippa of charges arising from an April 18, 2023 demonstration at the University of Pittsburgh's O'Hara Student Center against a transphobic speaker, during which a single firework was set off near a police line. Pleaded guilty; surrendered June 30, 2023 to begin a 60-month federal sentence — among the harshest handed down for a U.S. anti-fascist defendant since the post-J20 cases.

cases.charges: Conspiracy; obstruction of law enforcement during civil disorder; possession of unregistered destructive devices

name: Malik Muhammad
description: Black Palestinian-American anarchist and antifascist from Portland, Oregon, serving one of the longest sentences handed down to a 2020 George Floyd uprising defendant. Pleaded guilty in September 2022 to 14 felony counts after throwing Molotov cocktails and explosive devices toward Portland Police officers and at the East Portland Police Precinct during a September 5, 2020 protest. Sentenced to 10 years (state and federal sentences running concurrently) at Oregon's Snake River Correctional Institution.

cases.charges: Attempted assault on a public safety officer; riot; unlawful manufacture of a destructive device; first-degree arson (14 felonies)

name: Fran Thompson
description: Long-time Nebraska and Missouri environmental and animal-liberation organizer who fought campaigns against a factory egg farm and a low-level nuclear waste facility before being charged in the 1996 fatal shooting of a man who broke into her Missouri home. Supporters say she shot in self-defense; the trial court refused to allow her to present a self-defense plea. Convicted of second-degree murder and sentenced to life. Held at the Women's Eastern Reception, Diagnostic and Correctional Center.

cases.institution_name: Women's Eastern Reception, Diagnostic and Correctional Center

name: Jesse Cannon
description: San Diego antifascist, hip-hop artist, park defender, and longtime community organizer. Served a five-year California sentence stemming from a January 9, 2021 antifascist defensive action in Pacific Beach during which Cannon and others confronted Proud Boys and other far-right groups, plus a 2023 incident defending Chicano Park / Barrio Logan from outside threats. Took a non-cooperating plea; sentenced to five years (two and three running concurrently). Released August 2025 after serving his full term.

cases.charges: Assault, vandalism, brandishing a concealed firearm, use of a deadly weapon
cases.sentence: 5 years California state prison (2 + 3 concurrent); released August 2025

name: Calla Walsh
description: Massachusetts-based Palestine-solidarity activist and one of the Merrimack 4 — Calla Walsh, Bridget Shergalis, Paige Belanger, and Sophie Ross — who on November 20, 2023 carried out a Palestine Action-style direct action against an Elbit Systems of America facility in Merrimack, New Hampshire. The four smashed windows, set off smoke flares, and doused the company logo with fake blood to protest Elbit's manufacture of weapons systems used in the Israeli bombardment of Gaza. New Hampshire prosecutors initially threatened sentences of up to 37 years on felony sabotage charges; ultimately convicted of misdemeanor riot and criminal mischief. Walsh served 60 days at Valley Street Jail with a 24-month suspended sentence.

cases.charges: Riot; criminal mischief (Elbit Systems direct action, Nov 20 2023)
cases.sentence: 60 days Hillsborough County jail + 24-month suspended sentence
